meta.title: HSE Healthcare — Infection Prevention & Control Plan
meta.subtitle: Ward 4B, IPC programme review
meta.author: HSE Healthcare
meta.deid_notice: All personnel and patients are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This review baselines the Ward 4B infection prevention and control (IPC) plan, mapping each transmission route to its control and assurance owner. Hand-hygiene audit compliance is below target.

sections.caption: IPC plan — transmission route to control to owner

rows.title: Hand-hygiene compliance below target
rows.description: Audited compliance of 82% is below the 95% target on the night shift.

rows.title: Isolation-room pressure not logged daily
rows.description: Negative-pressure verification log has gaps on weekends.

rows.control: Point-of-care alcohol-gel dispensers at every bay

rows.action: Reinstate night-shift hand-hygiene audits and feedback
Fit a pressure alarm and automated log to the isolation room

sections.text: Do not use the isolation room for an airborne case until daily negative-pressure verification is reliably logged.

items: IPC code of practice (health and social care)
